conversation.content: I'm making that Thai basil chicken tonight. Can you help me remember the steps?
Sure! What recipe are you following?
The one from that cooking channel. So first I mince 4 cloves of garlic and slice 3 Thai chilies.
Got it, garlic and chilies prepped. What's next?
Fry the garlic and chilies in oil until fragrant, then add 400 grams of ground chicken. Cook until browned.
Okay, garlic and chilies first then the chicken.
Then the sauce: 2 tablespoons oyster sauce, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon fish sauce, and a teaspoon of sugar.
Classic combo. And the basil goes in last?
Exactly. Big handful of Thai basil, stir it in and kill the heat immediately. Serve over jasmine rice with a fried egg on top.
Sounds delicious. The fried egg on top really makes it.

queries.query: What was the sauce mixture again?
How much chicken did I say I was using?

conversation.content: New year, new goals. I'm training for the Portland half marathon in May.
That's awesome! Have you run a half before?
I did a 10K last year but nothing longer. My target time is under 2 hours.
Under 2 hours for a first half is ambitious but doable. What's your training plan?
Following the Hal Higdon novice plan. 4 runs a week with a long run on Sundays.
Solid choice. That plan has a great progression.
Training update! I ran 8 miles yesterday. Longest run ever for me. Felt great but my left knee is a bit sore.
8 miles is a big milestone. Is the knee something new?
It's been bugging me the last couple weeks actually. I'm icing it after runs. Might need to get new shoes.
New shoes might help a lot. How old are your current ones?
Probably 400 miles on them. Yeah I should replace them. I run in Brooks Ghost.
400 miles is right at the replacement point. Get a fresh pair and that knee might feel much better.
Ran 10 miles today! New shoes made a huge difference. Knee feels fine now.
That's great progress! And good to hear about the knee.
At this rate I might actually hit sub-2 hours. My pace was 8:45 per mile today which is right on target.
8:45 pace at 10 miles is very promising for a sub-2 half. You're on track.

queries.query: How is my running training going?
ground_truth: training for Portland half marathon in May
bonus_inferences: had knee issue that resolved with new shoes

conversation.content: I'm exhausted. Long day.
Rough one? What happened?
Nothing dramatic just a lot of meetings. The parking garage elevator was broken again so I had to take the stairs. 7 flights.
Seven flights! That's a workout on its own.
Tell me about it. Oh by the way, I decided to go back to school. I'm applying to the part-time MBA program at State University.
That's a big decision! What's driving that?
I want to move into leadership eventually and I feel like the MBA will open doors. Plus my company will cover 80% of tuition.
80% tuition coverage is amazing. When would you start?
Fall semester if I get in. Application deadline is April 15th. Anyway I need to figure out dinner. I think there are leftover enchiladas.
Leftover enchiladas sound perfect for a tired evening. Good luck with the MBA application!
Thanks. Also my neighbor's dog won't stop barking. It's been going on for hours. I might leave a note.
That's really annoying. A polite note might help.

queries.query: Am I working on any big life plans?
ground_truth: applying to part-time MBA at State University

conversation.content: Had dinner with the old college crew tonight. First time we all got together since October.
That's been a while! Who was there?
Me, Diego, Fatima, and Wes. We went to that Ethiopian place downtown, Desta.
Nice choice. How is everyone doing?
Diego just got promoted to senior engineer at Datadog. He's been there 3 years now.
Good for Diego! That's a solid company.
Fatima is finishing her PhD in computational biology at MIT. She's defending her thesis in April.
April defense — that's coming up fast. She must be stressed.
She seemed tired but excited. And Wes — oh man, Wes is getting married in September. His fiancée's name is Ling. She's a veterinarian.
Wedding season! A veterinarian — that's cool. How did they meet?
Through Diego actually! Ling is Diego's neighbor and he introduced them at a barbecue last summer.
Diego playing matchmaker. That's a great story.
Right? We're all going to the wedding. Diego is the best man. Fatima and I are doing a joint toast.
That sounds like it's going to be a really special day for your whole group.

ground_truth: Wes is getting married in September
fiancée is Ling who is a veterinarian

queries.query: What are my college friends up to?
ground_truth: Diego promoted to senior engineer at Datadog
Fatima defending PhD thesis at MIT in April
bonus_inferences: user and Fatima doing joint toast at wedding

conversation.content: Crazy morning. Jenkins build is broken because someone merged without tests.
Ugh. Do you know who merged it?
Tyler. He knows better too. I reverted his PR already but CI is still queued.
At least the revert is in. Anything else going on?
Yeah, client demo at 2pm. Need to prep the staging environment. The demo is for Nakamura Corp, our biggest prospect this quarter.
Big demo. What do they need to see?
The new dashboard and the API v3 integration. Rachel is leading the demo but I need to handle the technical Q&A.
So staging prep, technical Q&A support for Rachel. Got it.
Also the sprint retro moved to 4pm. And I need to submit my expense report by end of day or finance will yell at me again.
Packed schedule. Demo at 2, retro at 4, expense report by EOD.
Oh and I'm interviewing a backend candidate at 11. Name's Jordan. Resume looked strong — ex-Stripe, 5 years experience.
Lots on your plate. Interview at 11, demo at 2, retro at 4.
Welcome to Mondays. Oh one more thing — we're switching from Datadog to Grafana for monitoring. Migration starts next week.
That's a big infrastructure change. Who's leading the migration?
I am, unfortunately. First task is exporting all our custom dashboards. Should take about 2 weeks total.
Two weeks for a monitoring migration is tight. At least you know both platforms.

queries.query: What do I have going on at work today?
ground_truth: client demo at 2pm for Nakamura Corp
